intro: A Pivot Joint allows one bone to rotate or turn around another.
notes: Atlas (C1): The first vertebra, holds up the head.
Axis (C2): The second vertebra, has a peg to turn on.

hotspots.label: Atlas (C1)
hotspots.description:
The Atlas is the first cervical vertebra. It is a ring of bone that supports the skull.

It has no body and no spinous process.
hotspots.examTip: It is named after the Greek Titan 'Atlas' who held up the world.

hotspots.label: Axis (C2)
hotspots.description:
The Axis is the second vertebra. It has a vertical peg called the 'Dens' or 'Odontoid Process'.

The Atlas rotates around this peg.
hotspots.examTip: This mechanism allows you to turn your head side-to-side (shake 'No').

hotspots.label: Rotation
hotspots.description: In a pivot joint, a ring rotates around a peg (or a peg inside a ring).
hotspots.examTip: Another example is the joint between the Radius and Ulna near the elbow.
